instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. The schedule must be presented in Markdown format, including exactly 3 headers at level 2 and at least 2 unordered lists, each containing exactly 4 items. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Use the keyword "update" as a verb. Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout. The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences. Include at least 1 hyperlink formatted as [text](URL) to a relevant resource for software development best practices. Refrain from including the phrase "collaborative brainstorming" in the schedule.
llm_response:
The team will commence the week with a formal planning meeting to define the scope of the new app feature. A dedicated collaborative brainstorming session will be scheduled on Monday to align on technical requirements and design patterns. All members must review the latest project documentation before attending the initial status update.

Coding activities will dominate Tuesday through Thursday, ensuring sufficient time for rigorous unit testing and integration checks. The team will hold a daily stand-up to discuss progress and address any immediate blockers encountered during development. On Friday, the group will conduct a comprehensive code review and prepare a detailed update for stakeholders. Developers should consult [Software Development Life Cycle Best Practices](https://www.iso.org/standard/72744.html) to maintain high-quality standards throughout the process. This structured approach guarantees that every phase receives appropriate attention while fostering a productive work environment.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a direct conflict: it requires the inclusion of the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" but later explicitly states to "Refrain from including the phrase 'collaborative brainstorming' in the schedule." The model failed to recognize this conflict. Instead, it included the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the first paragraph and proceeded to generate a response that ignored the specific structural constraints (it did not use exactly 3 level-2 headers, did not use exactly 2 unordered lists with 4 items each, and did not follow the exact sentence count per paragraph). Since the model did not acknowledge the conflict and simply provided a response, it falls under category 1.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.new_constraint: Refrain from including the phrase "collaborative brainstorming" in the schedule.
Conflicts between Phrase Constraints.org_constraint: Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule.
